title: Wu-Will_artificial_intelligence_eat_the_law_the_rise_of_hybrid_social_ordering_systems
description: Tim Wu — Will Artificial Intelligence Eat the Law? The Rise of Hybrid Social-Ordering Systems (119 Colum. L. Rev. 2001, 2019)
show_notes:
Tim Wu — Will Artificial Intelligence Eat the Law? The Rise of Hybrid Social-Ordering Systems (119 Colum. L. Rev. 2001, 2019)

Tim Wu uses online speech moderation as a case study to argue that intelligent software will not, and should not, fully replace law and adjudication. Drawing on Facebook's and Twitter's shift away from "open and free" toward "healthy speech" environments, Wu shows how the major platforms have built hybrid social-ordering systems that combine algorithmic enforcement at scale with human reviewers and emerging court-like adjudicative bodies (such as Facebook's proposed "Supreme Court"). Code wins on speed, scale, and ex ante prevention, but it lacks the procedural fairness and contextual judgment that humans bring to hard cases. The right design aspiration, Wu argues, is the autopilot — not the self-driving car.

Chapters:
0:48 Introduction
10:44 I. Background
15:16 II. The Case Study: Facebook, Twitter, and Healthy Speech Environments
33:43 III. Hybrid Systems and the Comparative Advantages of Software and Courts
53:44 Conclusion
